question_text: Which of the following correctly describes what happens when white light passes through a colour filter?

options.label: A
options.text: All colours of light are absorbed equally

options.label: B
options.text: The filter absorbs certain colours and allows others to pass through

options.label: C
options.text: The filter creates new colours that were not present in the original light

options.label: D
options.text: The filter reflects all colours back to their source

correct_answer: The filter absorbs certain colours and allows others to pass through
explanation: A colour filter works by absorbing (stopping) certain colours of light while letting other colours pass through. The colour we see is the light that passes through the filter. For example, a red filter absorbs all colours except red, so red light passes through and we see red.
